steps.title: Use the vertex
steps.content: A horizontal line intersects a parabola exactly once when it passes through the vertex. For $y=-x^2+7x-76$, the vertex x-coordinate is found with the formula below.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content:
Here, $a=-1$ and $b=7$.
$$x=-\frac{7}{2(-1)}=\frac{7}{2}$$
Now evaluate the parabola at that x-value.
$$-(\frac{7}{2})^2+7(\frac{7}{2})-76=-\frac{49}{4}+\frac{98}{4}-\frac{304}{4}=-\frac{255}{4}$$
A graph shows that only this horizontal line touches the parabola once. The answer is D.
